lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Layers exist that have no lyr:layer descriptions
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Every layer used in the spec has a lyr:layer element with at least an onboard mode
lyr:mode.pr:p: For each layer namespace found in the spec's schemas, create a lyr:layer element describing it. Declare at minimum an onboard mode with prerequisites, conditions, and guidance. Register the layer description file in the index.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: A new specification is being created
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: An index.xml exists listing all spec files with correct spec identity and version
lyr:mode.pr:p: Create the index.xml manifest file. Declare the spec identity (spec:spec attribute) and version (spec:version). Add file elements for each spec file as they are created. The index defines the specification boundary and ID uniqueness domain.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: A new specification is being created
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: A revision.xml exists with at least one named revision snapshot
lyr:mode.pr:p: Create revision.xml with an initial named revision (e.g., draft-1). This snapshot anchors artifact mappings to a specific point in the spec's evolution. Set the timestamp and reference the index.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: The project has version-controlled source code
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All repositories referenced by artifact mappings have vcs:git declarations with correct remote URLs
lyr:mode.pr:p: Declare each git repository that will be referenced by artifact mappings. Use vcs:git with the remote URL and default branch. The element ID will be referenced by art:artifact/@repo attributes.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Source code or documentation exists to describe
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Every major module and component has a pr:section with title, shortdesc, and descriptive paragraphs
lyr:mode.pr:p: Create spec files organized by topic (one file per major module or component). Each file gets pr:section elements with meaningful titles and descriptions. Start with high-level architecture, then drill into subsystems. Use pr:shortdesc for scannable summaries.
Write prose that explains intent and design rationale, not just what the code does. Focus on "why" over "what".

lyr:mode.name: brainstorm
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: A new feature or component is being designed
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: New sections describe the feature's purpose, behavior, and design rationale
lyr:mode.pr:p: Write prose sections describing the new feature before code exists. Capture intent, constraints, and expected behavior. These sections become the foundation for implementation.

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Code modules exist without corresponding prose sections
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All code modules have prose descriptions
lyr:mode.pr:p: Identify code modules that lack prose coverage. Create sections for each, capturing what the module does and why it exists.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Domain concepts exist in the codebase
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All domain concepts have trm:term definitions with precise, unambiguous definitions
lyr:mode.pr:p: Scan code identifiers, comments, documentation, and prose sections for domain concepts. Extract each into a trm:term element with a canonical name and precise definition.

pr:ol.pr:li: Inventory naming patterns in source files
Group related terms into semantic clusters
Write trm:term elements with definitions that distinguish each term from similar concepts
Add trm:ref elements in prose wherever terms are used

lyr:mode.name: brainstorm
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: New domain concepts are being introduced
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: New terms are defined before implementation begins
lyr:mode.pr:p: When designing a new feature, identify new domain concepts that need controlled definitions. Define terms before writing code so that implementation uses consistent vocabulary.

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Code uses domain terms not yet defined in the spec
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: No undefined domain terms remain in the codebase
lyr:mode.pr:p: Search for domain-specific identifiers and concepts in the code that lack trm:term definitions. Add missing definitions.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Prose sections exist that need topic classification
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Every prose section has an org:concept, org:task, or org:reference classification with a purpose statement
lyr:mode.pr:p: Classify each prose section by topic type. Concepts explain ideas. Tasks describe system behavior. References provide lookup information. Add org:concept, org:task, or org:reference elements with purpose statements explaining the classification.

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Prose sections exist without topic classification
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All sections are classified
lyr:mode.pr:p: Find prose sections missing topic typing. Add the appropriate org:concept, org:task, or org:reference with purpose.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Multiple spec nodes exist that should be semantically linked
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: No isolated nodes remain in the connectivity graph (every node has at least one relation)
lyr:mode.pr:p: Add typed relations between spec nodes. Use depends-on for prerequisites, refines for parent-child, precedes for ordering, implements for spec-to-code concepts, and references for cross-references. Check connectivity with the connectivity command and iterate until no isolated nodes remain.

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Isolated nodes exist in the connectivity graph
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Zero isolated nodes in connectivity analysis
lyr:mode.pr:p: Run connectivity analysis. For each isolated node, identify its semantic relationships to other nodes and add appropriate rel:relation elements.

lyr:mode.name: review
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Spec has been modified since last review
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Connectivity analysis shows no isolated nodes and relation types accurately reflect semantic meaning
lyr:mode.pr:p: Review the connectivity graph for accuracy. Verify relation types match the actual semantic relationships. Add missing relations. Remove incorrect ones.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Architectural decisions have been made that should be recorded
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All significant architectural decisions have dec:decision records with status, alternatives, and rationale
lyr:mode.pr:p: Identify architectural decisions in the codebase (framework choices, data model decisions, API design choices). Create dec:decision records with status, rejected alternatives, and rationale for each.

lyr:mode.name: brainstorm
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: A design decision needs to be made
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: The decision is recorded with alternatives and rationale
lyr:mode.pr:p: When facing a design choice, create a dec:decision record in proposed status. Document alternatives with rationale. Update status to accepted once decided.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: External references (RFCs, papers, standards, documentation) are relevant to the project
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All referenced external materials have src:source declarations with proper classification
lyr:mode.pr:p: Identify external references mentioned in code comments, docs, or design discussions. Create src:source declarations with URLs, type classification, and optional cached content hashes.

lyr:mode.name: brainstorm
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: A feature design needs an implementation plan
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Implementation plan exists with items, acceptance criteria, and test references
lyr:mode.pr:p: Create a pln:plan element with title, overview, and itemized steps. Each item should have acceptance criteria. Link items to spec nodes they implement. Set status to proposed until work begins.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Code files and spec nodes both exist
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Every spec node has an art:mapping with valid hashes and precise line ranges. Coverage analysis shows zero unmapped nodes.
lyr:mode.pr:p: Link each spec node to its implementing code. Create art:mapping elements with spec-ref (node ID, revision, node-hash) and artifact (repo, path, line ranges, content hash).

pr:ol.pr:li: Scan source files for functions and modules corresponding to spec nodes
Create art:mapping elements with precise line ranges (prefer under 30 lines per range)
Run --fix-node-hash and --fix-artifact-hash to compute SHA-256 hashes
Run --coverage to verify zero unmapped nodes

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Unmapped spec nodes or drifted mappings exist
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Zero unmapped nodes and zero drifted mappings
lyr:mode.pr:p: Run drift and coverage analysis. For drifted mappings, update line ranges and re-fix hashes. For unmapped nodes, create new art:mapping elements.

lyr:mode.name: review
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Artifact mappings exist that may need quality improvement
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All mappings have precise line ranges, correct coverage levels, and descriptive notes
lyr:mode.pr:p: Review artifact mappings for quality. Check that line ranges are precise (not overly broad), coverage levels are accurate, and notes describe what the code implements. Fix drift and improve granularity where needed.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Spec nodes exist that need machine-readable descriptions
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Every spec node has an llm:node description. Every schema has an llm:schema description.
lyr:mode.pr:p: Add llm:node descriptions for each spec node, explaining what it means, how it relates to other nodes, and guidance for interpreting or modifying it. Add llm:schema descriptions for each layer namespace.

lyr:mode.name: catch-up
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Spec nodes exist without llm:node descriptions
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All nodes have descriptions
lyr:mode.pr:p: Find spec nodes missing llm:node descriptions and add them. Focus on nodes that are most important for LLM comprehension.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: External content needs to be referenced with integrity guarantees
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All external content references have cnt:content elements with valid hashes
lyr:mode.pr:p: For external content that other layers reference (test fixtures, data files, cached external documents), create cnt:content elements with URLs and content hashes for integrity verification.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Tests exist or need to be defined for spec verification
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: Key spec requirements have corresponding test definitions with appropriate types (manual, vectors, property, program, witness)
lyr:mode.pr:p: Define test specifications for key requirements. Choose the appropriate test type: program for executable tests, vectors for input/output pairs, property for invariants, manual for procedures requiring human judgment, witness for external verification.

lyr:mode.name: onboard
lyr:mode.lyr:condition: Python source code exists in the project
lyr:mode.lyr:acceptance: All public Python APIs have py:module, py:class, and py:function descriptions with parameter types and return types
lyr:mode.pr:p: Describe the Python API surface. Create py:module elements for packages, py:class for classes, and py:function/py:method for callables. Include parameter lists, return types, and raised exceptions.

rel:relation.rel:note: Terminology extraction requires prose to exist first, as terms are derived from and referenced within prose content.
Topic typing classifies prose sections, so prose must exist before organization can be applied.
Artifact mappings link spec nodes (primarily prose sections) to code, so spec nodes must exist first.
LLM descriptions reference spec nodes, which are primarily defined in the prose layer.
Artifact mappings reference VCS repositories, so repo declarations must exist first.
Artifact mappings pin to revisions, so at least one revision must exist.

spec:clayers.llm:node: The self-referential meta-layer: describes the layer layer itself. Its onboard mode tells orchestrators how to create lyr:layer instances for layers that lack descriptions.
The prose layer is the foundation that most other layers build on. Onboarding it first ensures spec nodes exist for terminology extraction, topic typing, relation building, and artifact mapping.
The artifact layer depends on the most other layers (prose, terminology, organization, VCS, revision) because it creates the bridge between spec and code. It is typically the last onboarding phase.
